tickets.title: Data + Memory Architecture — P1-P4 Progressive Alignment
tickets.description: Atlas to review the Data and Memory Architecture document (Phase 4 spec from Ken email 'DATA+MEMORY ARCHITECTURE', sent 2026-05-04). Align the P4 architecture to the P1-P4 progressive build phases defined in the EA roadmap. Output: phased data/memory architecture plan showing what gets built in each phase, dependencies, and gates between phases.
tickets.notes: Assigned to Atlas. Source doc saved to docs/Phase4_DataMemory_Architecture.md (from email 2026-05-04, 20.8KB). Atlas to run after TKT-0103 completes. | Groomed 2026-05-08 14:28 AEST. Add-on lenses confirmed 14:40 AEST: (1) Data classification + use (Client/Internal/System/Operational/Repository/Backup), (2) Data type (relational/structured/unstructured/vector/blob), (3) Additional considerations (encryption, real-time, batch, caching, retention policy), (4) Physical infrastructure architecture (storage type, location, performance tier). Atlas to cover all 4 lenses per phase across P1-P4. Firing Atlas now. | Ken decisions 2026-05-08 15:00 AEST: (1) Anthropic DPA deferred — P1 acceptable, no client data. Gate: must confirm before P2 live. (2) Embedding: nomic-embed-text 768-dim CONFIRMED. (3) P3 REDEFINED — not licensed product. New phase definitions: P2=SaaS individual agents, P3=SaaS company multi-agents (shared context + data), P4=Enterprise/FSI unchanged. DataMemory_P1P4_Roadmap.md requires amendment — P3 section must be rewritten. Flag to Atlas. | All decisions resolved 2026-05-08 15:19 AEST. Final: (1) Anthropic DPA deferred to P2 gate, (2) nomic-embed-text 768-dim confirmed, (3) P3=commercial tier label, (4) P2 isolation=RLS from day one, (5) P3 trigger=formal ROI checklist required — Ken skeptical of P3 value given maintenance cost. Strategic note: P4 enterprise may prefer physical/in-house deployment over P3 SaaS multi-agent — P3 may be skipped entirely in practice. TKT-0046 queued as next Atlas task.

tickets.title: Model3-Policy: Define SOPs and domain boundaries for all Tier 3 agents
tickets.description: AC for TKT-0103. Define formal SOPs and domain boundaries (Model3-Policy) for all Tier 3 (Yoda-Manage-Passthrough) agents: Spark, Ahsoka, Atlas, Thrawn, Lando, Mon Mothma, Krennic. Document: scope limits, what requires Ken/Angie approval, reporting cadence, escalation path, failure handling. Output: Model3-Policy.md in docs/. Ken to confirm before policy is live.
tickets.notes: Design consideration added 2026-05-08 14:49 AEST (Ken): Should Atlas have governance/assurance accountability over all architecture sub-agents and related architectural work? If yes, Atlas moves beyond Tier 3 passive passthrough — gains an assurance role, reviewing outputs of Thrawn, Lando, Mon Mothma, and any future architecture agents to ensure alignment. Implications: (1) Atlas tier classification may need revisiting — hybrid Tier 2/3? (2) What triggers Atlas review — all outputs or selective? (3) How does this interact with Yoda's coordination role? (4) Does Atlas assure just architecture quality, or also governance compliance? DECIDE AS PART OF TKT-0105 GROOMING. Do not implement before Ken confirmation.

tickets.title: Model3-Policy: Apply to existing Tier 3 agents, review and confirm with Ken
tickets.description: AC for TKT-0103. Once Model3-Policy.md is approved (TKT-0105), apply the SOPs and domain boundaries to each existing Tier 3 agent (Spark, Ahsoka, Atlas, Thrawn, Lando, Mon Mothma). Update each agent's SOUL.md/RULES.md to reflect their formal domain boundary and escalation path. Review each agent with Ken before marking as operationalised. Gate: TKT-0105 must be complete and approved first.

tickets.title: Agent Governance — Squad Model (Tier 5+): Ephemeral Dev/Delivery Squads
tickets.description: Forward-looking governance model for when AInchors spins up ephemeral agent squads for development and delivery work. May require a new tier beyond the current 0-4 framework. Questions to resolve: What is a squad? How are they composed (temporary multi-agent teams)? Who owns squad governance — Yoda or Atlas? How does a squad spin up and tear down? What's the approval gate? How does squad output get assurance-checked before merging back to main? TRIGGER: Review and define BEFORE any development squad work begins. Do not activate until development sprint work is imminent.

tickets.title: Document Generation Pipeline — DOCX/XLSX/PPTX/PDF (Ahsoka Q1 blocker)
tickets.description: Build reusable document generation pipeline for Ahsoka. Required to produce consulting proposals, discovery reports, data exports, and presentations. Ahsoka cannot deliver client outputs without this. Libraries: python-docx (DOCX), openpyxl+pandas (XLSX), python-pptx (PPTX), fpdf+reportlab (PDF). Deliverables: install script, generate-doc.sh wrapper, 4 AInchors-branded templates, test output for each format.

tickets.title: Agile PM / Scrum Master / Agile Coach Agent — Design and Build
tickets.description: Dedicated agent to own sprint facilitation, velocity tracking, QBR packs, epic/milestone management, dependency tracking, and Agile coaching for clients and business users. Three modes: (1) Scrum Master — sprint ceremonies, blockers, velocity, retrospectives; (2) Agile PM — epic tracking, capacity planning, QBR prep, dependency management; (3) Agile Coach — working with external clients and business users to run their own delivery framework. Proposed name: Cassian Andor. Governance: Tier 3 (Yoda-Manage-Passthrough). TRIGGER: multiple concurrent client projects OR external users running sprints OR squad model activated (TKT-0107) OR Yoda PM overhead >40%. REVIEW: July 2026 QBR. Do not build before review.

tickets.title: Process Documentation Framework — User Guides for Business Stream and Clients
tickets.description: Lando (BPM agent) to own process documentation for all product capabilities and features shipped. Documents must be ready BEFORE each release, written for business users and clients (non-technical). Current Notion content is technical and only fit for Ken. Need: (1) Process document template/standard (what goes in a user guide, style, format); (2) Per-capability user guides produced ahead of P2 releases; (3) Integration with sprint/release cycle — Definition of Done must include user doc ready; (4) Delivery format TBD (Notion public page, PDF, HTML). Covers: Nexus features, agent capabilities, Citadel portal, Ahsoka consulting outputs. Assign: Lando. Governance: Tier 3 (Yoda-Manage-Passthrough). Review: as part of P2 release planning.

tickets.title: Angie Agile + Nexus Working Guide — Delivered via Aria
tickets.description: Produce a practical guide for Angie on: (1) How to work within the AInchors Agile framework — sprint cycles, how to raise requests, what to expect each week, how her work gets prioritised; (2) How to work with Nexus multi-agent system — what Aria can do, how to instruct agents, what Ahsoka delivers, how to get the most out of the platform; (3) How to plan and execute business work efficiently using the agents — maximising output, knowing when to delegate vs decide, how to track progress. Delivered BY Aria TO Angie (Angie's pace — not pushed). Format: conversational, non-technical, practical. Lando owns process doc structure. Aria delivers and coaches. DEPENDENCY: TKT-0110 (process doc framework) for template/format standard. Part of Angie onboarding Stage 2+ completion.

tickets.title: obs-collector: cron run failures not captured in obs.db
tickets.description: cron-health-check.sh detects cron consecutiveErrors but does not feed results into obs.db. obs-collector.sh has no check against cron-health-state.json. Result: cron job failures are invisible to the standup 24h error count and obs trend dashboard. Kimi RTB cron failed silently (Day 15) — not surfaced in standup. Fix: add check in obs-collector.sh to read cron-health-state.json failures array and log each as obs event type=cron_run_fail level=ERROR source=cron-health.

tickets.title: Fallback alert channel — API-independent notification path
tickets.description: INC-20260509-001 prevention gap: Telegram alerting depends on Anthropic API — same API it monitors. When API hits /bin/zsh, alerts go silent. Need secondary alert path (local script → SMS or email via secondary provider e.g. Twilio/Postmark) that fires when primary API is degraded.
